pages.raw_text:
Governor Mead is in the city to-
day, having arrived this morning
from Everett, where he attended
the fair. Major Browne, the gov
ernors secretary, is also here and
this morning went to Occur
d'Alene, Idaho. The governor is
here for the purpose of completing
the appointment of Spokane dele
gates to the Boise irrigation con-
gress. He spent the day in COM
pany with Secretary Monroe and
members of the chamber of com
merce comforting on this matter.


L. c. Rohrabacher, secretary OF
the publicity committee of the Spo-
kane chamber of commerce. is on
the Sound endeavoring tn enlist
commercial bodies there in the
Spokane plan to populate the rural
districts of the northwest. He has
requested the commercial bodies to
each send delegates to the confer
ence of the Washington Publicity
bureau, which will meet in Spo-
kane September rs.
